qa_pairs.question: How do you include a single command within a wiki using the provided templates?
qa_pairs.answer: To include a single command within a wiki, you should use the `{{Command}}` template. This template detects bash syntax, allowing you to pass the command as its argument, for example: `{{Command|cd src; make && make install; cd ..}}`.

qa_pairs.question: When using the `{{Command}}` template in a wiki, how do you correctly include special characters like the equality sign (=) or the pipe symbol (|) within a command?
qa_pairs.answer: Since the `{{Command}}` template is interpreted by the wiki, special characters like the equality sign (`=`) and the pipe symbol (`|`) must be escaped. To include an equality sign, use the meta-template `{{=}}`. To include a pipe symbol, use `{{!}}`.

qa_pairs.question: How can you include a set of multiple commands, each on a separate line, within a wiki using the available templates?
qa_pairs.answer: To include a set of commands, each on a separate line, you can use the `{{Commands}}` template. Each command should be placed on its own line and prepended with the `|` character, as shown in this example: `{{Commands|cd src|make|make install|cd ..}}`.
